title: Rattle Configuration Recommendation
description: Output of the rattle-suggest-config workflow. Describes a BOM-aware product configuration ready to be applied via rattle-config-builder. Field names and types match the backend create-side Pydantic schemas where possible; downstream apply-config resolves `*_name` references to ids.

notes.description: Optional soft warnings the AI wants to bubble up (e.g. assumed-default placeholders).

decimal_string.description: Backend serialises prices as decimal strings (Numeric(10,2) → string). Accept either numeric or canonical 2-decimal string.

bom_rules.description: Optional BOM lines. Each rule maps to a PartPlacement (Part↔Area edge) or BomItem (Parent↔Child edge) write. Use `area_name` to scope a placement to a specific area; omit it for parent↔child BOM items.

forbidden.description: Forbidden option pairs for this product. Maps to ReplaceOptionConstraintsRequest.forbidden (POST /constraints atomic-replace; the body field is 'forbidden', not the legacy 'forbidden_pairs' or 'pairs').

key.description: Custom key. Discouraged unless the tenant memory profile explicitly opts in (minimal-keys rule).

price_overrides.description: Map of area_name → option_name → price for per-area price overrides. Maps to PUT /options/{id}/area-config?area_id=… writes (AreaConfigUpdateRequest.price). The wider override set (option_description, recommended, is_numbered, number_min/max/step/unit, option_key) lives on each option's `area_overrides` field below.

key.description: Custom key. Discouraged unless the tenant memory profile explicitly opts in (minimal-keys rule).

is_numbered.description: Set true to model a numeric-input option (e.g. count, length). Required when bom_rules reference this option via option_scalings.

price_scalings.description: Quantity-driven PRICE scaling for numbered options. Same descriptor shapes as option_scalings (legacy bare numeric, ratio {opt, part}, range {areas: [{min, max, part}]}). Keys are stringified option ids (the option's own id at apply time).

area_overrides.description: Per-area overrides beyond simple price. Each entry maps to ensure_area_config (PUT /options/{id}/area-config?area_id=…). The 9 REST-overridable fields are: price, option_key, option_description, recommended, is_numbered, number_min, number_max, number_step, number_unit.

bom_rule.description: A variant-BOM line. Two flavours: PartPlacement (set `area_name` and omit `parent_part_name`) and BomItem (set `parent_part_name` and omit `area_name`). Use `usage_subclauses` for conditional inclusion and `option_scalings` for quantity scaling.

parent_part_name.description: Parent part for a BomItem (Parent↔Child edge). Mutually exclusive with area_name.

area_name.description: Area for a PartPlacement (Part↔Area edge). Mutually exclusive with parent_part_name.

usage_subclauses.description: Full DSL: see skills/rattle-bom-builder/references/usage-subclauses.md. Recommendation-time entries can use a simplified per-option shape (apply-config resolves option_name → option_id):

anyOf.description: Full clause shape (operator + groupSelections / areaStatuses / areaSubclauses). Pass-through to the backend.

option_scalings.description: Quantity-scaling dict. Three descriptor shapes: legacy bare numeric, ratio {opt, part}, range {areas: [{min, max, part}]}. Keys are stringified option ids (apply-config resolves names to ids when the recommendation uses {option_name, descriptor} entries).

forbidden_pair.description: Pair-level forbidden combination. Apply-config resolves option names to ids and submits ALL pairs for the product in ONE atomic POST /constraints (body field 'forbidden').

constraint_rule.description: Conditional rule. The runtime evaluator (app/utils/constraint_solver._rule_active and ForbiddenRule.violates) consumes a single object {requires: [<clause>...], invalid: [<option_id>...]} — NOT an array, and NOT the legacy {if, then} shape.

area_name.description: Optional area scope (matches ForbiddenRule.area_id; null for product-wide rules).

requires.description: Array of clauses. ALL must be satisfied for the rule to fire. Each clause uses anyOf / allOf / groupSelections (apply-config resolves option_name lists to ids).

invalid.description: Option ids (or option names — apply-config resolves) that become forbidden when 'requires' is satisfied.
